start.speaker: Narration
start.text: [i]The briefing room. All seven crew members present. The holographic display shows a station—corporate, fortified, wrong. This is it. The job that changes everything.[/i]

narration_war_room.speaker: Narration
narration_war_room.text: [i]The table is scarred alloy. Seven chairs, none matching. Elia stands at the head, hands flat on the surface. The holo-projector hums—blue light carves a rotating schematic into the dark. Aurum Extraction Station K-12. Four levels. Thirty-seven rooms. One objective. Everyone knows the math: this is the hardest thing they've ever done together.[/i]

elia_opens.speaker: Elia
elia_opens.text: Floor check. Everyone's here. Good. [i][She activates the display.][/i] This is the target. Aurum Extraction forward station. Four levels, heavy security, civilian workers mixed with corporate enforcement.

elia_opens_2.speaker: Elia
elia_opens_2.text: No rehearsal. No second pass. We get one window and we use it or we lose Avyanna's legal protection permanently. [i][She meets each pair of eyes in turn.][/i] So I need everyone sharp. Questions held until intel review is done.

vesper_posture.speaker: Narration
vesper_posture.text: [i]Vesper sits with her legs crossed, rifle case leaning against her chair. Her fingers rest on the stock—not nervous, but listening through it, the way a violinist touches strings.[/i]

rho_posture.speaker: Narration
rho_posture.text: [i]Rho leans back, arms folded across the chest plate he never fully removes. His grin is present but dimmed—the kind he wears when he's already doing math on casualties.[/i]

nyx_posture.speaker: Narration
nyx_posture.text: [i]Nyx sits furthest from the projector, hood drawn, staff across her knees. The Lattice wards in the station schematic pulse faintly and she tracks them the way a hawk tracks movement in grass.[/i]

jalen_posture.speaker: Narration
jalen_posture.text: [i]Jalen has three screens open on the table, each running different data feeds. His eyes move faster than his hands. He hasn't looked up since the schematic loaded.[/i]

elisira_posture.speaker: Narration
elisira_posture.text: [i]Elisira stands behind her chair, not sitting. Arms at her sides. Still as architecture. Whatever she's thinking, it's behind walls older than the station they're about to crack open.[/i]

avyanna_posture.speaker: Narration
avyanna_posture.text: [i]You sit at the table's edge. The shard at the base of your skull hums—faint, steady, like a second heartbeat. You are the reason for all of this. The weight of that hasn't lessened.[/i]

elia_begins_briefing.speaker: Elia
elia_begins_briefing.text: Jalen. Intel first. Give us everything.

jalen_intel_start.speaker: Jalen
jalen_intel_start.text: [i][Tapping screens, pulling data into the holographic display.][/i] Aurum Extraction Station K-12. Officially registered as a mineral processing outpost. Unofficially—

jalen_intel_2.speaker: Jalen
jalen_intel_2.text: —it's a data nexus. Primordial extraction samples, Lattice research logs, and personnel records for every corporate black-site operative in the sector. All of it scheduled for transfer to Compact jurisdiction in six hours.

jalen_intel_3.speaker: Jalen
jalen_intel_3.text: [i][He zooms the schematic.][/i] Four levels. Sublevel is maintenance and utilities—low security, mostly automated. Ground floor is civilian operations and processing. Second floor is corporate offices and the comm array. Top floor—

jalen_intel_4.speaker: Jalen
jalen_intel_4.text: [i][His voice drops.][/i] Top floor is the data core. That's where the extraction records live. Hardened servers, physical airgap, no remote access. To get the data, someone has to be in the room.

jalen_timeline.speaker: Jalen
jalen_timeline.text: We have a six-hour window. After that, the transfer convoy arrives and it's out of reach. [i][He looks grim.][/i] This is our only shot.

jalen_tech_detail.speaker: Jalen
jalen_tech_detail.text: Internal comms run on a closed frequency—military-grade encryption. I can crack it, but I'll need forty seconds of uninterrupted access to a relay node. There's one on each floor.

jalen_layout_detail.speaker: Jalen
jalen_layout_detail.text: Elevator system is centrally controlled—once they lock it down, you're taking stairs or making your own paths. Emergency bulkheads on every floor can seal in under three seconds. [i][He pauses.][/i] If they seal the data core, we need shaped charges or a very creative Lattice operator to get through.

elisira_intel_add.speaker: Elia
elisira_intel_add.text: Elisira. You said you had intercepted comms.

elisira_comms_1.speaker: Elisira
elisira_comms_1.text: [i]She steps forward. A data chip appears in her hand like a card trick.[/i] Three days of encrypted traffic. Corporate internal. I broke it this morning.

elisira_comms_2.speaker: Elisira
elisira_comms_2.text: They know someone's coming. Not us specifically—but they've raised security posture. Double shifts, additional automated defenses on sublevels two and three. [i][Beat.][/i] And they've requested something called a 'Lattice containment package.'

nyx_reacts_containment.speaker: Nyx
nyx_reacts_containment.text: [i]Sharp intake of breath.[/i] Containment package. That's Writbound hardware.

elisira_comms_3.speaker: Elisira
elisira_comms_3.text: [i]Flat.[/i] Correct. The comms reference 'specialized personnel' arriving with the convoy. If that means Writbound operatives, our six-hour window is also our window before this becomes a fundamentally different kind of fight.

elisira_comms_4.speaker: Elisira
elisira_comms_4.text: One more thing. The station commander's name is Harlan Dreeve. Former Compact military, discharged for 'excessive initiative.' [i][The faintest curl of contempt.][/i] He'll fight smart. He won't surrender.

elisira_inside_knowledge.speaker: Elisira
elisira_inside_knowledge.text: I've been inside corporate operations. Their doctrine is containment first, elimination second. They'll try to lock you in before they try to kill you. [i][She looks at Elia.][/i] The bulkheads are the real weapon. Not the guards.

elia_thanks_intel.speaker: Elia
elia_thanks_intel.text: [i]Nodding.[/i] Good. That's the landscape. Now—threat assessment. Rho, what are we walking into?

rho_assessment.speaker: Rho
rho_assessment.text: Security is military-grade. Automated turrets, reinforced bulkheads, probably a few surprises we haven't mapped. [i][His grin is sharp.][/i] Standard nightmare scenario.

rho_assessment_2.speaker: Rho
rho_assessment_2.text: Ground floor: light infantry. Corporate enforcement—body armor, sidearms, crowd-control training. They're used to scaring miners, not fighting operators. We cut through them fast.

rho_assessment_3.speaker: Rho
rho_assessment_3.text: Second floor: heavy infantry. Military-grade hardware, tactical training, probably wired reflexes on the squad leads. These ones know how to fight in corridors. [i][He leans forward.][/i] They'll use the bulkheads to channel us into kill zones.

rho_assessment_4.speaker: Rho
rho_assessment_4.text: Top floor data core: unknown. Could be light, could be a fortress. Jalen's data doesn't cover internal defenses on that level. [i][He looks at the schematic.][/i] I don't like unknowns.

rho_assessment_turrets.speaker: Rho
rho_assessment_turrets.text: Automated turrets on sublevels are Kessler-pattern—rotating, thermal-tracking, suppressive fire. Not accurate, but they don't need to be. They pin you down while the infantry flanks. Standard corporate layered defense.

rho_assessment_final.speaker: Rho
rho_assessment_final.text: [i][Settling back.][/i] Total estimated hostiles: forty to sixty. We're seven. That's roughly eight to one. [i][The grin widens.][/i] I've had worse odds. But not by much.

vesper_intel.speaker: Vesper
vesper_intel.text: Intel suggests they're preparing to move [b]Primordial extraction samples[/b]—including records of Site K-9. If those records reach Compact jurisdiction, Avyanna becomes a legal recovery target.

vesper_sniper_assessment.speaker: Vesper
vesper_sniper_assessment.text: Sight lines inside the station are short—fifteen meters maximum on most corridors. That limits my effectiveness indoors. [i][She touches the rifle case.][/i] I'm most useful at the extraction point or in any open areas. Inside the core, I switch to close-quarters protocols.

vesper_civilian_concern.speaker: Vesper
vesper_civilian_concern.text: [i]Quieter.[/i] Civilian workers. Jalen said there are processing staff on the ground floor. Non-combatants. [i][She looks at Elia.][/i] Rules of engagement need to be clear. We're not them.

elia_roe.speaker: Elia
elia_roe.text: [i]Firm.[/i] No civilian casualties. Period. If a civilian is between you and the objective, you find another path. Anyone who violates that answers to me personally.

rho_roe_response.speaker: Rho
rho_roe_response.text: [i]No argument. Just a nod.[/i] Copy. Clean hands.

nyx_concern.speaker: Nyx
nyx_concern.text: The station has Synthetic wards. Strong ones. I can disrupt them, but it'll take time—and it'll light us up like a flare. Once I start, stealth is over.

nyx_lattice_detail.speaker: Nyx
nyx_lattice_detail.text: The wards are layered. Outer shell is standard—I can peel it in under a minute. But there's something underneath. Deeper. [i][She frowns.][/i] The signature doesn't read like corporate tech. It reads like Lattice architecture. Old Lattice.

nyx_lattice_detail_2.speaker: Nyx
nyx_lattice_detail_2.text: If they've built Writbound containment into the station's ward structure, I'm not just fighting their security—I'm fighting the building itself. [i][She meets your eyes.][/i] And Avyanna, your shard will react. Count on it.

nyx_writbound_warning.speaker: Nyx
nyx_writbound_warning.text: Writbound operatives are not soldiers. They're channelers—Lattice practitioners who've bonded with containment entities. Think of a Synthetic ward that can think, adapt, and hunt. [i][Her jaw tightens.][/i] I've fought one before. I won. Barely.

bee_first_analysis.speaker: BEE
bee_first_analysis.text: {BEE:: lattice-resonance-detected | station-wards-anomalous | sub-harmonic matches primordial extraction signature—this facility was built around something, not on top of it}

avyanna_bee_reaction.speaker: Narration
avyanna_bee_reaction.text: [i]The shard pulses—warm, insistent. BEE's analysis bleeds through like ink through paper. The station wasn't just built to store data. It was built to contain something. And that something is still there.[/i]

player_ask_bee.speaker: Avyanna
player_ask_bee.text: BEE says the station was built around something. The Lattice signature underneath the wards—it's not security. It's containment. The facility is a cage.

crew_bee_reaction.speaker: Narration
crew_bee_reaction.text: [i]Silence. The kind that means everyone just recalculated the risk.[/i]

bee_objective_analysis.speaker: BEE
bee_objective_analysis.text: {BEE:: objective unchanged | data core still primary target | additional variable: contained entity may react to shard proximity | recommend: caution in sublevel approach}

nyx_responds_bee.speaker: Nyx
nyx_responds_bee.text: [i]To Avyanna, low.[/i] Your ghost has good instincts. If there's something caged under that station, my ward disruption might wake it up. [i][She doesn't look afraid. She looks fascinated.][/i] We should plan for that.

elia_threat_summary.speaker: Elia
elia_threat_summary.text: So. Forty to sixty hostiles. Military-grade security. Automated defenses. A station commander who won't quit. Possible Writbound operatives. Lattice wards that fight back. And something caged underneath it all. [i][She exhales.][/i] Anyone want to walk?

elia_threat_pause.speaker: Narration
elia_threat_pause.text: [i]No one moves. No one speaks. The offer is genuine—Elia would let them go. But they're here.[/i]

rho_stays.speaker: Rho
rho_stays.text: [i]Cracking his knuckles.[/i] I walked into worse on Meridian. At least this time I like the people I'm walking with.

text_variants.rho_bonded: [i]Cracking his knuckles, then catching your eye with something warmer than his usual grin.[/i] I walked into worse on Meridian. But I didn't have family watching my back then.

vesper_stays.speaker: Vesper
vesper_stays.text: [i]Not looking up from her rifle.[/i] Don't insult me by asking.

text_variants.vesper_bonded: [i]Not looking up from her rifle, but her voice carries something almost soft.[/i] You already know the answer. Don't waste breath asking.

nyx_stays.speaker: Nyx
nyx_stays.text: Old Lattice architecture? This might be the most interesting thing I've done in years. [i][Her smile is sharp and genuine.][/i] I'm in.

text_variants.nyx_bonded: Old Lattice architecture and a crew worth protecting? [i][Her smile reaches her eyes—rare and genuine.][/i] I'm exactly where I should be.

jalen_stays.speaker: Jalen
jalen_stays.text: [i]Without looking up from his screens.[/i] My code is already halfway through their encryption. Walking away now would be professionally embarrassing.

text_variants.jalen_bonded: [i]He looks up—actually looks up—and meets your eyes.[/i] I built half these systems for people like them. It's time I took them apart for people like us.

text_variants.elisira_bonded: [i]One word, but she holds Elia's gaze when she says it, and something passes between them that isn't language.[/i] No.

elia_acknowledges_crew.speaker: Elia
elia_acknowledges_crew.text: [i]The faintest smile—pride, grief, gratitude braided together.[/i] Good. Then let's talk strategy.

strategy_approach_choice.speaker: Elia
strategy_approach_choice.text: Two ways to hit this. We go quiet—infiltration, disable security in sequence, reach the data core before they know we're inside. Or we go loud—simultaneous breach, overwhelming force, speed over subtlety. [i][She looks at you.][/i] Avyanna. You've seen how we work. What's your read?

approach_player_choice.speaker: Narration
approach_player_choice.text: [i]The crew watches you. This isn't delegation—it's trust. Elia is testing whether you can read the board and call the play.[/i]

choices.text: [Stealth Approach] Infiltration. Disable their eyes before they see us coming.
[Assault Approach] Overwhelming force. Hit them so hard they can't organize a response.
[Hybrid Approach] Both. Stealth team disables security while assault team waits at the breach point. Synchronized.

stealth_choice_check.speaker: Narration
stealth_choice_check.text: [i]You outline the stealth approach. Elia watches you draw the route on the holographic display—maintenance tunnels, service corridors, security blind spots. This requires tactical precision.[/i]

stealth_choice_success.speaker: Narration
stealth_choice_success.text: [i]Your hand moves with confidence across the display. Entry points, timing windows, fallback routes—the plan unfolds like a mechanism.[/i]

stealth_crew_react_good.speaker: Vesper
stealth_crew_react_good.text: [i]Leaning forward. Impressed.[/i] That's clean. Service corridor approach avoids every turret emplacement on levels one and two. Where did you learn to read floor plans?

jalen_stealth_good.speaker: Jalen
jalen_stealth_good.text: [i]Pulling up schematics.[/i] She's right—the maintenance tunnels connect to the elevator shaft on sublevel two. I can loop their internal cameras from there. We'd have maybe eight minutes of invisibility.

elisira_stealth_good.speaker: Elisira
elisira_stealth_good.text: [i]Appraising.[/i] Silent approach favors precision eliminations. Suppressed weapons, close work. [i][She touches the pistol at her hip.][/i] This is what I'm built for.

elia_approves_stealth.speaker: Elia
elia_approves_stealth.text: [i]Nodding slowly.[/i] Good call. Stealth gives us the element of surprise and keeps civilians out of crossfire. The risk is if we're detected early—then we're deep inside with no support.

stealth_nyx_concern.speaker: Nyx
stealth_nyx_concern.text: One problem. My ward disruption isn't quiet. The moment I start peeling their Lattice defenses, every Synthetic sensor in the station knows. [i][She thinks.][/i] Unless I work from inside the ward structure itself. Slower, but silent.

stealth_rho_concern.speaker: Rho
stealth_rho_concern.text: [i]Frowning.[/i] Stealth means I can't bring the rotary cannon. Corridors, suppressed weapons, careful footwork. [i][Beat.][/i] I can do it. I just want everyone to know I'm doing it under protest.

stealth_choice_failure.speaker: Narration
stealth_choice_failure.text: [i]You sketch the approach on the display, but the routing is rough—a blind spot in your timing leaves a gap in camera coverage. Vesper catches it.[/i]

vesper_corrects_stealth.speaker: Vesper
vesper_corrects_stealth.text: [i]Leaning forward, tapping the display.[/i] That corridor has overlapping camera arcs. We'd need to loop them simultaneously, not in sequence. [i][She adjusts the route.][/i] But the core idea is sound. Let me clean it up.

stealth_corrected.speaker: Elia
stealth_corrected.text: The instinct is right—stealth approach. Vesper and Jalen will refine the routing. [i][She looks at you.][/i] Good eye for the entry point. The execution needs work, but that's what a crew is for.

assault_choice_check.speaker: Narration
assault_choice_check.text: [i]You outline the assault approach. Breach points, suppressive fire lanes, room-clearing sequences. The display fills with red arrows. This requires understanding of force application.[/i]

assault_choice_success.speaker: Narration
assault_choice_success.text: [i]The plan unfolds across the display like a controlled detonation—every breach timed, every fire lane covered, every fallback accounted for.[/i]

rho_assault_good.speaker: Rho
rho_assault_good.text: [i]Grinning—full, genuine, dangerous.[/i] Now you're speaking my language. Simultaneous breach on three entry points—they can't reinforce all of them. We pick the weakest and punch through.

elia_assault_good.speaker: Elia
elia_assault_good.text: Speed and violence of action. If we hit hard enough, their command structure collapses before Dreeve can organize a response. [i][She traces the assault route.][/i] Thirty seconds from breach to second floor. Ninety seconds to the data core.

vesper_assault_concern.speaker: Vesper
vesper_assault_concern.text: [i]Cool.[/i] Civilian casualties become a real risk in a direct assault. Ground floor processing workers—if they panic, they run into our fire lanes.

jalen_assault_solution.speaker: Jalen
jalen_assault_solution.text: I can trigger a fire alarm on the ground floor sixty seconds before breach. Civilian evacuation protocols are automatic—they'll clear the processing areas before we enter.

elia_approves_assault.speaker: Elia
elia_approves_assault.text: [i]Decisive.[/i] Do it. Assault approach, pre-breach civilian evacuation. The trade-off is they'll know we're coming sixty seconds early. [i][She looks at Rho.][/i] Can we still achieve surprise?

rho_assault_answer.speaker: Rho
rho_assault_answer.text: They'll know something is wrong. They won't know what until we're already inside. Sixty seconds of confusion is enough.

assault_choice_failure.speaker: Narration
assault_choice_failure.text: [i]Your fire lanes overlap in two places—friendly fire risk. Rho catches it before you finish.[/i]

rho_corrects_assault.speaker: Rho
rho_corrects_assault.text: [i]Not unkind, but direct.[/i] Those two corridors create a crossfire problem—our own people get caught in the overlap. [i][He adjusts the display.][/i] Stagger the breach by four seconds. Team one enters here, team two sweeps in from the service corridor after the first volley clears.

assault_corrected.speaker: Elia
assault_corrected.text: Right idea, needs calibration. Rho's adjustment fixes the crossfire issue. [i][She nods at you.][/i] The aggression is correct. Sometimes speed is the safest option.

hybrid_choice_check.speaker: Narration
hybrid_choice_check.text: [i]A combined approach. The most complex option—two teams, synchronized timing, overlapping contingencies. This requires exceptional tactical awareness.[/i]

hybrid_choice_success.speaker: Narration
hybrid_choice_success.text: [i]The display lights up like a symphony score—two movements, perfectly timed, each dependent on the other. Infiltration team disables cameras and turrets. Assault team breaches the moment the lights go dark.[/i]

elia_hybrid_impressed.speaker: Elia
elia_hybrid_impressed.text: [i]Both eyebrows raised.[/i] That's... ambitious. And elegant. Stealth team cripples their eyes, assault team hits while they're blind. [i][She traces the timing on the display.][/i] The synchronization has to be perfect. Five seconds off and the stealth team is exposed with no support.

vesper_hybrid.speaker: Vesper
vesper_hybrid.text: [i]Studying the display, nodding slowly.[/i] It solves both problems. Stealth for the approach, force for the objective. And it gives us a fallback—if the stealth team is detected, assault team breaches early.

elisira_hybrid.speaker: Elisira
elisira_hybrid.text: [i]For the first time, something like respect in her voice.[/i] This is how the Compact's best units operate. Paired operations. [i][She looks at you.][/i] You've either studied their doctrine or you have very good instincts.

hybrid_choice_failure.speaker: Narration
hybrid_choice_failure.text: [i]The concept is strong, but your timing windows don't align—the stealth team would be in position three minutes before the assault team is ready. Elisira sees the gap.[/i]

elisira_corrects_hybrid.speaker: Elisira
elisira_corrects_hybrid.text: [i]Stepping to the display.[/i] The idea is correct. The timing isn't. [i][Her fingers adjust the sequence with surgical precision.][/i] Stealth team enters here. Assault team repositions to secondary breach point—closer approach, tighter window. Synchronization at T-minus-zero.

hybrid_corrected.speaker: Elia
hybrid_corrected.text: Hybrid approach. Elisira's timing fixes. This is the hardest option to execute, but it gives us the most flexibility. [i][She looks at you.][/i] Bold thinking. That's what we need.

approach_decided.speaker: Elia
approach_decided.text: Approach is set. Now the hard part. [i][She dims the display, leaving only the station outline.][/i] We split into two teams. This isn't optional—one team takes the data core, one team holds extraction. If the hold team fails, nobody gets out.

elisira_plan.speaker: Elisira
elisira_plan.text: [i]Single syllable, command weight.[/i] Split. [i][She gestures to the display.][/i] Four go in—fast, loud, direct assault on the data core. Three hold extraction point and provide fire support.

elisira_split_logic.speaker: Elisira
elisira_split_logic.text: Assault team needs a breacher, a ward specialist, a close-quarters fighter, and either tech support or precision fire. Hold team needs range, coordination, and someone who can anchor a defensive position against superior numbers.

crew_weighs_in_intro.speaker: Elia
crew_weighs_in_intro.text: Before we assign, I want everyone's input. Speak to your strengths and your limits. No ego. [i][She looks around the table.][/i] Vesper. Start.

vesper_weighs_in.speaker: Vesper
vesper_weighs_in.text: Indoor corridors limit my effective range. I'm strongest at extraction—long sight lines, elevated position, counter-sniper work. [i][She pauses.][/i] That said, if you need me inside, I can switch to close-quarters. I'm not useless at ten meters. Just... suboptimal.

vesper_weighs_in_2.speaker: Vesper
vesper_weighs_in_2.text: [i]Quieter.[/i] My concern is comms. If the assault team hits a bulkhead seal and I can't reach them—if I can't see them—I'm shooting blind. I need eyes on the team or I'm just a body holding a position.

rho_weighs_in.speaker: Rho
rho_weighs_in.text: I'm your breacher. That's not ego, that's physics—I'm the only one who can put a shaped charge through a reinforced bulkhead and walk through the hole. Put me on the assault team.

rho_weighs_in_2.speaker: Rho
rho_weighs_in_2.text: [i]Sobering.[/i] Limitation: I'm loud. If stealth is the priority, I need to stay quiet until the breach moment. I can do it—but everyone should know it goes against every instinct I have.

rho_weighs_in_3.speaker: Rho
rho_weighs_in_3.text: [i]Lower. Honest.[/i] And if we're talking limits—I can't protect everyone at once. In Meridian, I tried to cover two exits. Lost people at both. [i][He meets Elia's eyes.][/i] Don't split my attention. Give me one job and I'll do it.

nyx_weighs_in.speaker: Nyx
nyx_weighs_in.text: I have to be on the assault team. The data core will have the heaviest wards—if there are Writbound elements, that's where they'll be anchored. Without me, you hit that wall and stop.

nyx_weighs_in_2.speaker: Nyx
nyx_weighs_in_2.text: [i]Her eyes find you.[/i] And Avyanna's shard will react to the Lattice architecture. If she's on the assault team, I need to be there to manage the resonance. If she's not—I need to manage it remotely, and that's harder.

nyx_weighs_in_3.speaker: Nyx
nyx_weighs_in_3.text: Warning: once I engage their wards, I'm committed. I can't split my focus between offense and defense. Whoever's near me needs to keep hostiles off my back while I work. [i][She looks at Rho.][/i] That means you.

jalen_weighs_in.speaker: Jalen
jalen_weighs_in.text: I can go either way. Inside, I crack their security systems in real-time—cameras, bulkheads, automated defenses. Outside, I coordinate comms and run electronic warfare from the ship.

jalen_weighs_in_2.speaker: Jalen
jalen_weighs_in_2.text: Trade-off: if I'm inside, I'm vulnerable. I'm not a fighter. I'll need protection while I work. If I'm outside, I have the ship's full processing power but limited access—I can only crack systems I can reach through the comm relay.

jalen_weighs_in_3.speaker: Jalen
jalen_weighs_in_3.text: [i]Adjusting his glasses.[/i] Honestly? The data core's airgap means someone needs to be physically present to extract. If that someone isn't me, you need to give me five minutes to teach whoever goes in how to run the extraction protocol. It's not complicated. It's just specific.

elisira_weighs_in.speaker: Elisira
elisira_weighs_in.text: I know how corporate security thinks. I know Dreeve's likely deployment pattern. I know where his blind spots are because they're the same blind spots I exploited when I was inside the system. [i][Flat.][/i] Assault team.

elisira_weighs_in_2.speaker: Elisira
elisira_weighs_in_2.text: But I should also say this: if Dreeve recognizes me, it changes the calculus. He'll know our intel is deeper than surface level. He may trigger a data purge rather than let us take the records intact.

elisira_weighs_in_3.speaker: Elisira
elisira_weighs_in_3.text: [i]Meeting Elia's eyes.[/i] I'm a tactical asset and a potential liability. Use me where you think the math works.

elia_weighs_in.speaker: Elia
elia_weighs_in.text: I go where the crew needs me. Assault team wants a corridor fighter—that's me. Hold team wants an anchor who won't break—that's also me. [i][She looks at you.][/i] This is your call, Avyanna.

elia_split_question.speaker: Elia
elia_split_question.text: [i]Looking at you.[/i] Avyanna. You've been training with all of us. You know our strengths. [i][Beat.][/i] Who goes in, and who holds the line?

player_choice.speaker: Narration
player_choice.text: [i]All eyes on you. The weight of the decision is real. Who you send in determines tactics. Who holds the line determines if anyone gets out.[/i]

choices.text: [Assault: Elia, Elisira, Rho, Nyx | Hold: Vesper, Jalen, Avyanna]
[Assault: Elia, Vesper, Nyx, Avyanna | Hold: Elisira, Rho, Jalen]
[Assault: Elisira, Rho, Nyx, Jalen | Hold: Elia, Vesper, Avyanna]

assault_team_1.speaker: Elia
assault_team_1.text: [i]Nodding.[/i] Heavy assault. Rho breaches, I clear corridors, Elisira handles precision eliminations, Nyx disrupts their wards. Fast and brutal.

hold_team_1.speaker: Vesper
hold_team_1.text: [i]Calm.[/i] Jalen pilots extraction, I provide overwatch and counter-sniper. Avyanna coordinates comms and tracks hostiles. [i][She looks at you.][/i] You'll be our eyes.

team_1_reactions.speaker: Rho
team_1_reactions.text: [i]Grinning.[/i] This is going to be loud. I like it.

team_1_nyx_react.speaker: Nyx
team_1_nyx_react.text: Rho breaches, I follow through the gap. His explosions will mask my ward disruptions—by the time they realize I'm in their Lattice, it's already peeled. [i][She almost smiles.][/i] Symbiotic chaos.

team_1_elisira_react.speaker: Elisira
team_1_elisira_react.text: I'll handle Dreeve personally if we encounter him. [i][She checks her pistols—a gesture that's half maintenance, half ritual.][/i] He won't expect me. That advantage lasts exactly once.

team_1_jalen_react.speaker: Jalen
team_1_jalen_react.text: I'll run electronic warfare from the extraction point. Camera loops, comms jamming, bulkhead overrides—whatever the assault team needs, I'll push remotely. [i][He looks at Vesper.][/i] You and I need a shared tactical display.

team_1_vesper_avyanna.speaker: Vesper
team_1_vesper_avyanna.text: [i]To you.[/i] Avyanna. At the extraction point, your shard may pick up things Jalen's sensors miss. Lattice signatures, ward shifts, personnel movement. Trust what you feel. Report what you sense. [i][She holds your gaze.][/i] I'll handle the rest.

team_1_confirmation.speaker: Elia
team_1_confirmation.text: [i]To the assault team.[/i] We go fast. We go hard. We do not stop until we're in the data core. [i][To the hold team.][/i] You are our lifeline. If you fall, we die in there. [i][Beat.][/i] No pressure.

assault_team_2.speaker: Vesper
assault_team_2.text: [i]Raised eyebrow.[/i] Mixed range. Elia closes distance, I provide precision fire, Nyx handles wards, Avyanna... [i][She looks at you.][/i] You're coming in with us?

avyanna_in_assault.speaker: Avyanna
avyanna_in_assault.text: [i]Steady.[/i] I can track Lattice signatures. I'll know where the wards are before you hit them.

team_2_elia_react.speaker: Elia
team_2_elia_react.text: [i]Searching your face.[/i] You understand what going inside means. If the extraction point falls, we're trapped. If the wards react to your shard, you become a beacon.

team_2_avyanna_response.speaker: Narration
team_2_avyanna_response.text: [i]You nod. There's no bravery in it—just clarity. Your shard is the reason they built this cage. You should be the one to open it.[/i]

hold_team_2.speaker: Elisira
hold_team_2.text: [i]Flat.[/i] Rho breaches from range. Jalen coordinates timing. I handle counter-assault if they flank. [i][Beat.][/i] Surgical.

team_2_rho_react.speaker: Rho
team_2_rho_react.text: [i]Cracking his neck.[/i] Hold team with the big gun. Fine. Anyone comes near that extraction point, they meet the rotary cannon. [i][He glances at Elisira.][/i] Try to leave some for me.

team_2_jalen_react.speaker: Jalen
team_2_jalen_react.text: I'll run overwatch from the hold position. Full electronic warfare suite. [i][He looks at Nyx.][/i] When you hit the wards, I'll try to map the resonance pattern from outside. Might give you an edge.

team_2_confirmation.speaker: Elia
team_2_confirmation.text: [i]To Avyanna.[/i] Stay close to me. If it gets ugly, you run. Understood?

team_2_avyanna_final.speaker: Narration
team_2_avyanna_final.text: [i]You don't agree to run. You just nod. Elia sees the difference but doesn't press it.[/i]

assault_team_3.speaker: Elisira
assault_team_3.text: [i]Single nod.[/i] Precision strike. Rho breaches clean, Nyx disrupts wards without flare, Jalen maps optimal routes in real-time. No waste.

hold_team_3.speaker: Elia
hold_team_3.text: [i]Grim smile.[/i] Vesper and I hold extraction. Long-range dominance, close-quarters fallback. Avyanna coordinates. [i][She looks at you.][/i] This puts you in the most danger if they breach our perimeter.

team_3_vesper_react.speaker: Vesper
team_3_vesper_react.text: [i]Quiet.[/i] They won't breach. Not while I'm watching.

team_3_elisira_react.speaker: Elisira
team_3_elisira_react.text: Jalen—you're inside with me. I need real-time security overrides. Can you keep up?

team_3_jalen_react.speaker: Jalen
team_3_jalen_react.text: [i]A flash of something—pride, maybe, or stubbornness.[/i] I built systems like these. I'll take them apart faster than you can walk through them.

team_3_rho_react.speaker: Rho
team_3_rho_react.text: Clean breaches on Elisira's mark. Shaped charges, precision placement, minimal collateral. [i][He looks at Nyx.][/i] I'll make the holes. You do the magic.

team_3_nyx_react.speaker: Nyx
team_3_nyx_react.text: With Jalen mapping the ward architecture from inside, I can target specific nodes instead of brute-forcing the whole structure. More efficient. Less noise. [i][She looks satisfied.][/i] This is the smart play.

team_3_confirmation.speaker: Elia
team_3_confirmation.text: [i]To the assault team.[/i] Elisira has command inside. Follow her calls. [i][To you.][/i] Avyanna—you coordinate between teams. You're our bridge. If comms go down, you're the last thread holding this together.

final_briefing.speaker: Elia
final_briefing.text: [i]Standing.[/i] Everyone clear on roles? Questions? Concerns? [i][She looks around the table.][/i] Speak now.

final_questions_choice.speaker: Narration
final_questions_choice.text: [i]The table is quiet. But the silence is full of things unsaid.[/i]

choices.text: [Ask about contingencies] What if the assault team gets trapped?
[Ask about the entity] What if whatever's caged under the station wakes up?
[Ask about Dreeve] What do we know about the station commander?
[Stay silent] You've said enough. Let them prepare.

contingency_question.speaker: Elia
contingency_question.text: If the assault team is trapped behind a sealed bulkhead, the hold team has ninety seconds to reach them before the station's internal security converges. [i][She's thought about this.][/i] After ninety seconds, the assault team fights through or finds another way out. The hold team does not abandon the extraction point to rescue them.

contingency_hard_truth.speaker: Elia
contingency_hard_truth.text: [i]Harder.[/i] If the assault team cannot reach the data core and cannot retreat, the hold team extracts without them. That's the order. [i][She looks at each assault team member.][/i] You volunteered for the harder job. You knew the math.

rho_contingency.speaker: Rho
rho_contingency.text: [i]Quiet.[/i] Understood. But for the record—if it comes to that, I'll be the one holding the door so everyone else gets out. That's not heroism. That's physics. I'm the hardest to kill.

contingency_to_silence.speaker: Narration
contingency_to_silence.text: [i]No one argues with him. Not because they agree, but because they know he means it.[/i]

entity_question.speaker: Nyx
entity_question.text: [i]She's been waiting for someone to ask.[/i] If the contained entity reacts to our presence—specifically to Avyanna's shard—it could do several things. Amplify the station's wards. Disrupt Lattice operations on both sides. Or...

nyx_entity_detail.speaker: Nyx
nyx_entity_detail.text: ...it could try to communicate. Primordial entities aren't mindless. They're alien. If it recognizes the shard, it might reach for Avyanna. [i][She meets your eyes.][/i] If that happens, you tell me immediately. Don't try to manage it alone.

bee_entity_analysis.speaker: BEE
bee_entity_analysis.text: {BEE:: primordial-contact-protocol logged | host-protection: priority-absolute | note: if the cage opens, I will not be a passive observer}

entity_to_silence.speaker: Narration
entity_to_silence.text: [i]BEE's words settle into your bones. The shard pulses once—acknowledgment or warning, you're not sure which.[/i]

dreeve_question.speaker: Elisira
dreeve_question.text: Harlan Dreeve. Forty-seven. Former Compact Shock Infantry, decorated twice, court-martialed once. Discharged for executing prisoners during the Veritas Corridor incident. Aurum hired him the same week.

elisira_dreeve_2.speaker: Elisira
elisira_dreeve_2.text: He's competent, ruthless, and loyal to whoever signs his contract. He won't surrender because surrender means a tribunal—and he's already had one. [i][Her voice is flat, clinical.][/i] He'll fight to the last.

elisira_dreeve_3.speaker: Elisira
elisira_dreeve_3.text: [i]Softer—barely.[/i] I've met men like him before. They're not evil. They're empty. They filled the hole with duty and orders. Take away the structure and they collapse. [i][She looks at Elia.][/i] Don't try to reason with him. Just be faster.

dreeve_to_silence.speaker: Narration
dreeve_to_silence.text: [i]Elisira steps back. Whatever she left unsaid about her own time inside the system hangs in the air like smoke.[/i]

choices.text: [Ask another question]
[No more questions. Move on.]

crew_silence.speaker: Narration
crew_silence.text: [i]Silence. Not fear. Readiness. Everyone knows what's at stake.[/i]

bee_tactical_full.speaker: BEE
bee_tactical_full.text: {BEE:: tactical-summary compiled | threat-level: severe | crew-cohesion: high | probability-of-mission-success: 34.7% | probability-of-zero-casualties: 11.2% | personal-annotation: numbers lie—this crew defies probability}

avyanna_bee_private.speaker: Narration
avyanna_bee_private.text: [i]You feel BEE's analysis like a weight in your chest. Thirty-four percent. One in nine chance everyone comes home. The shard warms, and beneath the numbers, you sense something else—something BEE doesn't quantify. Belief.[/i]

elia_dismisses_briefing.speaker: Elia
elia_dismisses_briefing.text: Briefing's done. Four hours until insertion. Gear up, rest, do whatever you need to do. [i][She pauses.][/i] I want everyone back here in three hours for final checks.

crew_disperses.speaker: Narration
crew_disperses.text: [i]The crew rises. Chairs scrape. The holographic station rotates in empty blue light. But no one leaves immediately—they linger, finding reasons to stay close. This is the last quiet before the storm.[/i]

personal_moments_choice.speaker: Narration
personal_moments_choice.text: [i]The briefing room empties slowly. You have time to speak with someone before preparations begin.[/i]

choices.text: [Find Vesper] She's in the observation deck, cleaning her rifle.
[Find Rho] He's in the cargo bay, checking his charges.
[Find Nyx] She's in the engine room, meditating near the Lattice conduits.
[Find Jalen] He's at his workstation, running simulations.
[Find Elisira] She's in the armory, alone.
[Stay with Elia] She hasn't left the briefing room.

personal_vesper.speaker: Narration
personal_vesper.text: [i]The observation deck. Stars turn beyond the viewport. Vesper sits cross-legged on the floor, rifle disassembled across a cloth with the precision of a surgical kit. She doesn't look up when you enter. She knew you were there before the door opened.[/i]

vesper_personal_1.speaker: Vesper
vesper_personal_1.text: [i]Hands moving, reassembling by touch.[/i] You don't have to check on me.

text_variants.vesper_bonded: [i]Hands moving, but she shifts to make space beside her.[/i] I was wondering if you'd come.

vesper_personal_choice.speaker: Narration
vesper_personal_choice.text: [i]Her hands are steady. Her breathing is controlled. But there's a tension in her shoulders that the rifle cleaning isn't reaching.[/i]

choices.text: I'm not checking on you. I'm asking you to check on me.
Are you scared?
[Sit quietly beside her]

vesper_personal_honest.speaker: Vesper
vesper_personal_honest.text: [i]Her hands pause. Just for a moment.[/i] That's... unusually honest. [i][She looks at you.][/i] What do you need?

vesper_personal_direct.speaker: Vesper
vesper_personal_direct.text: [i]A breath of something that might be a laugh.[/i] Scared. That's a word for people who can still choose to leave. [i][She slots the barrel into place.][/i] I'm... aware of consequences.

vesper_personal_silent.speaker: Narration
vesper_personal_silent.text: [i]You sit. The stars turn. Vesper's hands continue their ritual. For a while, that's enough.[/i]

vesper_personal_depth.speaker: Vesper
vesper_personal_depth.text: [i]Quietly, after a long moment.[/i] I've been in operations where people died. I've been the reason some of them didn't. [i][She looks at the stars.][/i] But I've never done it with people I—

vesper_personal_catch.speaker: Vesper
vesper_personal_catch.text: [i]She stops. Recalibrates. When she speaks again, the sniper is back.[/i] People I've trained with this long. The calculus changes when you know someone's patterns. Their breathing. Their tells. [i][Beat.][/i] It makes you better. And it makes losing them worse.

vesper_personal_resolve.speaker: Vesper
vesper_personal_resolve.text: So no. I'm not scared. I'm precise. And tomorrow, precision keeps people alive. [i][She chambers a round—a sound like a period at the end of a sentence.][/i] Including you.

personal_rho.speaker: Narration
personal_rho.text: [i]The cargo bay. Rho sits surrounded by shaped charges, detonators, and enough ordnance to crack a small moon. His hands move with surprising delicacy—each charge measured, calibrated, placed in sequence.[/i]

rho_personal_1.speaker: Rho
rho_personal_1.text: [i]Without looking up.[/i] Pull up a crate. Just don't touch the blue ones. Those are temperamental.

text_variants.rho_bonded: [i]He looks up and smiles—not the sharp grin, but something quieter.[/i] Hey. Grab a seat. I could use the company.

rho_personal_choice.speaker: Narration
rho_personal_choice.text: [i]He handles explosives like a sculptor handles clay. There's reverence in it. And underneath the steady hands, something heavier.[/i]

choices.text: You mentioned Meridian. What happened there?
How do you stay calm before something like this?
[Help him sort the charges in silence]

rho_personal_meridian.speaker: Rho
rho_personal_meridian.text: [i]His hands slow. Not stop—slow.[/i] Meridian was a mining colony. Corporate dispute turned into a siege. Thirty of us went in to break it. Twelve came out.

rho_meridian_2.speaker: Rho
rho_meridian_2.text: [i]He picks up a charge, turns it in his hands.[/i] I tried to hold two positions at once. Ego. I was big enough, strong enough—I thought I could cover both. [i][His voice drops.][/i] I was wrong.

rho_personal_calm.speaker: Rho
rho_personal_calm.text: [i]He laughs—short, honest.[/i] Calm? I'm not calm. I'm focused. There's a difference. Calm means you think you're safe. Focused means you know you're not, and you're working the problem anyway.

rho_personal_help.speaker: Narration
rho_personal_help.text: [i]You sort charges by color, by weight, by the careful labels Rho has written in small, neat handwriting. He watches you handle them with respect and nods once—approval.[/i]

rho_personal_truth.speaker: Rho
rho_personal_truth.text: [i]Quieter than you've ever heard him.[/i] I like this crew. I like the way Elia runs things—no thrones, just floors. I like that Vesper insults me and means it as affection. I like that Jalen thinks his way out of problems I'd punch through.

rho_personal_truth_2.speaker: Rho
rho_personal_truth_2.text: [i]He meets your eyes.[/i] And I like that you're here. A kid with a shard in her skull and more courage than sense. [i][The grin comes back, but warmer.][/i] Reminds me why I stopped doing this for money.

personal_nyx.speaker: Narration
personal_nyx.text: [i]The engine room. The Lattice conduits pulse with subdued light—the ship's nervous system, half-alive, humming with potential. Nyx sits in the center of it, cross-legged, staff across her knees, eyes closed. The air around her tastes like static.[/i]

nyx_personal_1.speaker: Nyx
nyx_personal_1.text: [i]Eyes still closed.[/i] Your shard is loud today. I could feel you coming from two corridors away.

text_variants.nyx_bonded: [i]Eyes opening, and there's warmth in them.[/i] I was reaching for your shard without thinking. It's become a habit. You feel like a lighthouse.

nyx_personal_choice.speaker: Narration
nyx_personal_choice.text: [i]The conduits pulse in time with something—your heartbeat, or hers, or both.[/i]

choices.text: What does the station's Lattice architecture feel like from here?
You said you fought a Writbound before. Tell me.
[Sit with her. Match your breathing to the conduit pulse.]

nyx_personal_lattice.speaker: Nyx
nyx_personal_lattice.text: [i]She extends a hand, fingers splayed. The air between you shimmers.[/i] Feel it? Even from here—across void, across distance—the station's ward structure broadcasts. Cold. Geometric. Like a scream pressed into a grid.

nyx_personal_writbound.speaker: Nyx
nyx_personal_writbound.text: [i]Her eyes open. The memory is right there, behind them.[/i] Kallos Station. Three years ago. A containment entity had bonded with a security operative. He could reshape walls, seal doors with a thought, turn the architecture into a weapon. [i][She touches a scar beneath her collar.][/i] I won because I'm stubborn and he was overconfident. That's not a strategy I'd recommend.

nyx_personal_meditate.speaker: Narration
nyx_personal_meditate.text: [i]You sit. You breathe. The conduits pulse. And for a moment, through the shard, you feel what Nyx feels—the Lattice as a living thing, vast and old and full of doors.[/i]

nyx_personal_fear.speaker: Nyx
nyx_personal_fear.text: [i]After a silence that stretches like taffy:[/i] I'm not afraid of dying. I'm afraid of losing my connection. The Lattice is... it's how I understand the world. If those Writbound wards tear through my defenses—if they sever that—

nyx_personal_fear_2.speaker: Nyx
nyx_personal_fear_2.text: [i]She shakes her head.[/i] It would be like going deaf. Blind. Worse. It would be like forgetting that music exists. [i][She looks at you.][/i] Protect my back in there. Not my body—my focus. Keep them off me long enough to do my work.

personal_jalen.speaker: Narration
personal_jalen.text: [i]Jalen's workstation. Six screens, three keyboards, and a coffee cup that's been refilled so many times it's developed geological strata. His eyes move faster than his hands, and his hands are already blurring.[/i]

jalen_personal_1.speaker: Jalen
jalen_personal_1.text: [i]He holds up one finger—wait.[/i] ...[i]typing intensifies[/i]... [i]another thirty seconds[/i]... Done. Partial decryption of their secondary comm channel. [i][He spins his chair to face you.][/i] What's up?

text_variants.jalen_bonded: [i]He sees you and immediately saves his work—actually saves and closes a screen. For Jalen, that's the equivalent of a standing ovation.[/i] Hey. Come look at this.

jalen_personal_choice.speaker: Narration
jalen_personal_choice.text: [i]His desk is chaos with a system. Every piece of paper, every data chip, every tangled cable has a place he remembers even if no one else could find it.[/i]

choices.text: Can you walk me through the data extraction protocol? In case something goes wrong.
How are you holding up?
[Look at his simulations. Try to understand the numbers.]

jalen_personal_honest.speaker: Jalen
jalen_personal_honest.text: [i]He stops typing. Actually stops. His hands go still on the keys and he stares at them like they belong to someone else.[/i] Holding up. That's a generous way to describe what I'm doing. I'm running simulations because if I stop, I'll think about the fact that I built systems exactly like the ones we're about to break into.

jalen_personal_honest_2.speaker: Jalen
jalen_personal_honest_2.text: [i]He looks at you.[/i] I was corporate. Did you know that? Three years as a systems architect for Meridian Industrial. I built the security that kept people like us out. [i][He swallows.][/i] Now I'm on this side of the wall, and I keep wondering if someone like me is in there right now, building the thing that kills one of us.

jalen_personal_learn.speaker: Narration
jalen_personal_learn.text: [i]You lean in. The screens show probability cascades, security response models, timing optimizations. The numbers are dense, but patterns emerge—the way Jalen sees the world, translated into data.[/i]

jalen_personal_learn_2.speaker: Jalen
jalen_personal_learn_2.text: [i]He notices you reading. His expression shifts—surprise, then something warmer.[/i] You see the pattern? The security response clusters here—predictable, because corporate doctrine values consistency over creativity. That's their weakness. [i][He points.][/i] And that gap is where we get in.

jalen_personal_tech.speaker: Jalen
jalen_personal_tech.text: [i]He lights up—genuinely, completely. This is his language.[/i] Okay. The data core is airgapped—no wireless access. Physical terminal only. You'll see a console with a biometric lock. I'll spoof the biometrics remotely if I'm on the hold team, but if comms are down—

jalen_tech_detail_2.speaker: Jalen
jalen_tech_detail_2.text: —you use this. [i][He hands you a data chip, small and warm.][/i] Insert it into the console. It'll bypass the biometric and start the data pull. Takes ninety seconds. Don't remove it early or the files corrupt.

jalen_tech_check.speaker: Narration
jalen_tech_check.text: [i]He walks you through the process. Terminal identification, chip insertion, progress indicators. It's not complicated—but in a firefight, nothing is simple.[/i]

jalen_tech_success.speaker: Jalen
jalen_tech_success.text: [i]Surprised. Pleased.[/i] You picked that up fast. Most people need three run-throughs. [i][He leans back.][/i] If I go down, you can finish the extraction. That's... that's good to know.

jalen_tech_failure.speaker: Jalen
jalen_tech_failure.text: [i]Patient.[/i] Close. The sequence is insert-confirm-wait. Not insert-wait-confirm. Let's run it again. [i][He smiles.][/i] You'll get it. The important part is you asked.

jalen_personal_truth.speaker: Jalen
jalen_personal_truth.text: [i]After the tutorial, quieter:[/i] You know what scares me? It's not the guns or the wards. It's the data. What if we get in there and the records show something we can't undo? What if Avyanna's extraction history is worse than we think?

jalen_personal_truth_2.speaker: Jalen
jalen_personal_truth_2.text: [i]He takes off his glasses, rubs his eyes.[/i] I build systems. I believe in information. But some information breaks people. [i][He puts the glasses back on.][/i] I guess that's why we have each other. To hold the weight together.

personal_elisira.speaker: Narration
personal_elisira.text: [i]The armory. Elisira stands at the workbench, field-stripping her dual pistols with the mechanical grace of a concert pianist warming up. The room smells like gun oil and old metal. She doesn't acknowledge your entry.[/i]

elisira_personal_1.speaker: Elisira
elisira_personal_1.text: [i]Without turning.[/i] If you're here to ask if I'm alright, the answer is yes. If you're here for something else, say it.

text_variants.elisira_bonded: [i]She glances over her shoulder—brief, but warm enough to notice.[/i] You. Good. I wanted to say something before tomorrow.

elisira_personal_choice.speaker: Narration
elisira_personal_choice.text: [i]Her hands don't stop moving. Disassemble, clean, reassemble. A ritual older than the crew.[/i]

choices.text: You know Dreeve. Is this personal for you?
What were you, before?
[Pick up a cleaning kit and work on your own weapon beside her]

elisira_personal_dreeve.speaker: Elisira
elisira_personal_dreeve.text: [i]Her hands slow—not stop.[/i] I don't know Dreeve. I know the type. The machine made him and the machine will use him until he breaks. [i][She slots a component into place.][/i] That's not personal. That's pattern recognition.

elisira_personal_before.speaker: Elisira
elisira_personal_before.text: [i]A pause. The kind that means she's deciding how much truth to spend.[/i] I was a tool. A very expensive, very effective tool. I did what they pointed me at and I did it well. [i][Beat.][/i] That's not who I am now. But it's who I know how to be.

elisira_personal_parallel.speaker: Narration
elisira_personal_parallel.text: [i]You work side by side. The rhythm of maintenance is shared language—clean, inspect, assemble. After a while, the silence becomes comfortable. Elisira's shoulders drop a fraction. That's trust, from her.[/i]

elisira_personal_deeper.speaker: Elisira
elisira_personal_deeper.text: [i]After a long silence:[/i] I chose this crew. Not the other way around. Elia thinks she recruited me. She didn't. I watched them for weeks before I made contact. Measured their patterns. Their ethics. Their weaknesses.

elisira_personal_deeper_2.speaker: Elisira
elisira_personal_deeper_2.text: [i]She holds a pistol up to the light, checking the barrel.[/i] They're good people doing hard work without losing themselves. That's rare. That's worth protecting. [i][She looks at you sidelong.][/i] So is whatever's growing in you. The shard, the entity—BEE. Don't let anyone take it from you.

personal_elia.speaker: Narration
personal_elia.text: [i]The briefing room, emptied. The holographic station still rotates in blue light. Elia stands at the window, looking out at the dark. She's removed her blade harness—a thing she almost never does. She looks smaller without it.[/i]

elia_personal_1.speaker: Elia
elia_personal_1.text: [i]She knows you're there. She always does.[/i] Thought you'd go find the others first.

text_variants.elia_bonded: [i]She turns, and there's something unguarded in her expression. Just for a moment.[/i] I'm glad it's you.

elia_personal_choice.speaker: Narration
elia_personal_choice.text: [i]Without the harness, without the table between you, she's just a person carrying too much and pretending it weighs nothing.[/i]

choices.text: How do you carry this? Knowing you might send people to die?
Are we going to make it?
[Say nothing. Just stand beside her and look at the stars.]

elia_personal_weight.speaker: Elia
elia_personal_weight.text: [i]Long exhale.[/i] I don't carry it. I hold it. There's a difference. Carrying implies you can put it down. [i][She looks at her hands.][/i] I hold it because if I let go, someone else has to pick it up, and they might not be strong enough.

elia_personal_odds.speaker: Elia
elia_personal_odds.text: [i]She turns to face you. Direct.[/i] Honestly? I don't know. The math says no. The crew says yes. [i][A ghost of a smile.][/i] I've always bet on people over numbers.

elia_personal_quiet.speaker: Narration
elia_personal_quiet.text: [i]You stand together. The stars don't care about your plans. The dark doesn't know your names. But here, in this small room, two people look at the void and choose not to blink.[/i]

elia_personal_truth.speaker: Elia
elia_personal_truth.text: [i]Quietly. The voice she uses when the floor is just her and the truth.[/i] When I started this crew, I had a rule: don't love them. Keep it professional. Respect, competence, clear roles. Don't make it personal.

elia_personal_truth_2.speaker: Elia
elia_personal_truth_2.text: [i]She laughs—a short, broken sound.[/i] I failed that rule the first week. Rho made me laugh. Vesper made me think. Nyx made me wonder. Jalen made me hope. Elisira made me trust. And you—

elia_personal_truth_3.speaker: Elia
elia_personal_truth_3.text: [i]She stops. Starts again.[/i] You made me remember why I do this. Not the contracts. Not the survival. The [i]people[/i]. [i][She meets your eyes.][/i] So yes. We're going to make it. Because I refuse to lose any of you.

personal_return.speaker: Narration
personal_return.text: [i]You return to the common area. The ship hums around you—alive, waiting, faithful.[/i]

choices.text: [Speak with someone else]
[Head to the briefing room. It's time.]

three_hours_later.speaker: Narration
three_hours_later.text: [i]Three hours pass. Some of it is spent in silence. Some in the quiet work of preparation—weapons checked, charges calibrated, Lattice focuses aligned. The ship's corridors feel different now. Charged. Like the air before a storm knows what's coming.[/i]

equipment_check_intro.speaker: Narration
equipment_check_intro.text: [i]The gear-up room. Everyone present. The smell of gun oil, ozone, and the faint copper tang of charged Lattice components. Armor is donned in ritual silence.[/i]

equipment_vesper.speaker: Narration
equipment_vesper.text: [i]Vesper assembles Clarity—her primary rifle—with movements so practiced they're indistinguishable from breathing. Each component clicks into place: stock, barrel, scope, suppressor. She chambers a round and the sound is a full stop.[/i]

equipment_rho.speaker: Narration
equipment_rho.text: [i]Rho shrugs into his chest plate—scarred ceramic over ballistic weave, heavy enough to stop most things that would kill a normal person. He loads the rotary cannon's drum magazine with the tenderness of a parent tucking in a child. Shaped charges go into bandoliers. He doesn't count them. He knows.[/i]

equipment_nyx.speaker: Narration
equipment_nyx.text: [i]Nyx draws Lattice patterns on her forearms in conductive ink—temporary circuits that amplify her connection to the Threshold staff. The ink glows faintly, then settles into her skin like tattoos. She mouths words in a language that predates the Compact.[/i]

equipment_jalen.speaker: Narration
equipment_jalen.text: [i]Jalen straps a portable terminal to his forearm, connects three redundant comm units, and pockets seven data chips. He's already running diagnostics on each one. His sidearm goes into a hip holster—last, almost an afterthought.[/i]

equipment_elisira.speaker: Narration
equipment_elisira.text: [i]Elisira's preparation is the most economical. Two pistols—checked, loaded, holstered in a cross-draw rig. A thin-bladed knife in each boot. Light body armor under her jacket. She looks like she's going for a walk. That's the point.[/i]

equipment_elia.speaker: Narration
equipment_elia.text: [i]Elia belts her blade harness—twin short swords, balanced for corridor work. She tests the draw, once, twice, three times. The blades sing. She adds a compact sidearm, almost hidden. Last: she pulls her hair back and ties it. War face. Ready.[/i]

equipment_avyanna.speaker: Narration
equipment_avyanna.text: [i]You gear up. Light armor—you're not built for heavy combat yet, and the shard's weight is its own burden. A sidearm. A comm unit. Jalen's extraction chip in your inner pocket, close to your heart. And beneath it all, the shard pulses—steady, warm, aware.[/i]

bee_equipment_scan.speaker: BEE
bee_equipment_scan.text: {BEE:: host-systems nominal | shard-resonance stable | combat-readiness assessed | personal note: I will be with you in the dark places—you will not walk alone}

equipment_insight_check.speaker: Narration
equipment_insight_check.text: [i]As the crew finishes gearing up, you study their faces. Their movements. Something about the way they prepare tells you things words can't.[/i]

insight_success.speaker: Narration
insight_success.text: [i]You see it—the tells. Rho's grin doesn't reach his eyes. Vesper's breathing is too controlled, compensating for adrenaline. Jalen's hands are steady but he keeps touching his sidearm like he's reminding himself it's there. Elisira is the calmest, which means she's the most prepared for the worst. Nyx's conductive ink is slightly asymmetrical—she redrew the left arm pattern. Her writing hand shook.[/i]

insight_elia_detail.speaker: Narration
insight_elia_detail.text: [i]And Elia. Elia has checked her blades three times instead of the usual two. She's running the numbers in her head—who might not come back, and what she'll do if that happens. She's already carrying the grief of losses that haven't occurred yet.[/i]

insight_pass.speaker: Narration
insight_pass.text: [i]The crew is ready. Whatever they're feeling, they've packed it behind the armor and the weapons and the trained composure. Professionals.[/i]

waffle_climax_ready.speaker: Waffle.bat
waffle_climax_ready.text: [i]{WAFFLE.BAT// ALL_SYSTEMS: combat ready | CREW_VITALS: elevated but stable | PERSONAL_NOTE: bring everyone home. please.}[/i]

bubbles_climax_care.speaker: Bubbles
bubbles_climax_care.text: [i]Softly, through the briefing room speakers:[/i] I'll be watching. All of you. Come back safe.

cinnamon_climax_ops.speaker: Cinnamon
cinnamon_climax_ops.text: Ship locked for combat. All subsystems optimal. Go.

elia_speech_setup.speaker: Narration
elia_speech_setup.text: [i]The crew stands in the briefing room. Armed. Armored. Ready. The holographic station turns between them like a ghost they're about to make real. Elia steps to the head of the table. She doesn't lean on it this time. She stands straight.[/i]

elia_speech_1.speaker: Elia
elia_speech_1.text: I'm not going to give you a speech about duty. You know duty. You live it every day on this ship, in every job we take, in every choice we make to stay together when walking away would be easier.

elia_speech_2.speaker: Elia
elia_speech_2.text: I'm not going to tell you the odds. Jalen already ran them. They're bad. [i][A few grim smiles around the table.][/i] But we've beaten bad odds before. That's not luck. That's us.

elia_speech_3.speaker: Elia
elia_speech_3.text: What I will tell you is this: inside that station are records that will determine whether Avyanna lives free or gets dragged back into a cage. [i][She looks at you.][/i] She's one of us. That makes this simple.

elia_speech_4.speaker: Elia
elia_speech_4.text: [i]Harder. Commander voice.[/i] But I want to be clear about something. This is not a suicide mission. We go in, we get the data, we get out. If the objective becomes impossible, we abort. If someone goes down, we carry them. If we have to choose between the data and a life—

elia_speech_5.speaker: Elia
elia_speech_5.text: [i]She looks at each of them. One at a time. Taking the measure.[/i] —we choose the life. Every time. No data is worth more than any one of you. [i][Beat.][/i] Not even mine.

elia_speech_6.speaker: Elia
elia_speech_6.text: [i]Quieter now. Personal.[/i] I found each of you in different places. Different kinds of broken. Different kinds of strong. And we built something here—in this ugly ship, with these mismatched chairs, with a floor that holds because we [i]make[/i] it hold.

elia_speech_7.speaker: Elia
elia_speech_7.text: [i]Her voice is steady. Her eyes are not.[/i] I am proud. Of all of you. Of what we are. And I am asking you to walk into something dangerous, not because I have the right to ask, but because you've given me that right. Every day. By staying.

elia_speech_8.speaker: Elia
elia_speech_8.text: [i]She draws one blade—not a threat. A salute.[/i] So let's go take what's ours. And let's come home.

speech_reaction.speaker: Narration
speech_reaction.text: [i]For a moment, no one moves. Then Rho slams a fist against his chest plate—once, hard, resonant. Vesper nods. Nyx raises her staff. Jalen closes his screens. Elisira draws a pistol and holds it up, barrel to the ceiling. And you—[/i]

speech_player_choice.speaker: Narration
speech_player_choice.text: [i]The crew waits for you. The newest member. The reason for all of it.[/i]

choices.text: [Draw your weapon and raise it with the crew]
[Place your hand over the shard at your neck — your answer is in the pulse of it]
[Simply say: 'Floor holds.']

speech_response_weapon.speaker: Narration
speech_response_weapon.text: [i]You draw your sidearm and raise it. The gesture is understood—you are one of them. The weapon is a promise: you'll fight. You'll stay. You'll hold the floor.[/i]

speech_response_shard.speaker: Narration
speech_response_shard.text: [i]Your hand finds the shard. It pulses—once, strong, warm—and the Lattice conduits in the room flicker in response. BEE's presence fills the space between your heartbeats. You are not just crew. You are the reason the cage will open.[/i]

bee_speech_response.speaker: BEE
bee_speech_response.text: {BEE:: host-affirmation registered | crew-bond acknowledged | the floor holds because we hold it—all of us, living and lattice-born}

speech_response_words_react.speaker: Narration
speech_response_words_react.text: [i]Two words. But from you—the person they're risking everything for—they land like an oath. Elia's eyes shine. She nods once.[/i]

crew_response.speaker: Narration
crew_response.text: [i]The response is immediate, overlapping, muscle memory.[/i] 'Floor holds.'

elia_final_words.speaker: Elia
elia_final_words.text: We go in four hours. Gear up. Rest if you can. [i][She looks at each person.][/i] We're getting those records. And we're all coming home.

final_tactical_review.speaker: Narration
final_tactical_review.text: [i]The final hour. The crew runs through comms checks, weapon safeties, and one last review of the station schematic. Tension has crystallized into focus.[/i]

jalen_comms_check.speaker: Jalen
jalen_comms_check.text: Comms check. Assault team, channel alpha. Hold team, channel bravo. Emergency fallback on channel delta. [i][He taps his earpiece.][/i] If you hear static on alpha, switch to delta immediately. If delta goes down—

jalen_comms_check_2.speaker: Jalen
jalen_comms_check_2.text: [i]He pauses.[/i] If delta goes down, fall back to visual signals. Rho's flares. Vesper's tracer rounds. Nyx's Lattice pulse. We've drilled this. Trust the training.

vesper_final_review.speaker: Vesper
vesper_final_review.text: Overwatch positions mapped. Primary, secondary, and tertiary fallback points for extraction. [i][She looks at the schematic one final time.][/i] Sight lines confirmed. Counter-sniper protocols loaded. If they have marksmen, I'll find them first.

rho_final_review.speaker: Rho
rho_final_review.text: Breach charges calibrated. Three for bulkheads, two for emergency access, one reserve. [i][He holds up the last charge.][/i] This one's for something we haven't planned for. There's always something.

nyx_final_review.speaker: Nyx
nyx_final_review.text: Lattice disruption array is primed. I'll hit their outer wards the moment we're in range. The inner wards—the old ones—I'll assess in real-time. [i][She flexes her fingers and the conductive ink glows.][/i] I'm ready.

elisira_final_review.speaker: Elisira
elisira_final_review.text: Suppressed pistols, close-quarters loadout, Dreeve's likely command post flagged on the schematic. [i][She checks her watch—mechanical, analog, the only non-digital thing about her.][/i] Two minutes to insertion window.

bee_final_analysis.speaker: BEE
bee_final_analysis.text: {BEE:: final tactical assessment | all variables logged | crew cohesion: maximum observed value | probability models inadequate for this variable | conclusion: they will exceed expectations—they always do}

final_departure_narration.speaker: Narration
final_departure_narration.text: [i]The ship drops out of drift. The station appears in the viewport—a dark shape against darker void, running lights blinking like a mechanical heartbeat. It looks small from here. It won't feel small inside.[/i]

elia_go_order.speaker: Elia
elia_go_order.text: [i]One last look at her crew. Every face. Every name she carries.[/i] Teams to positions. Assault team forward. Hold team, secure extraction on my mark.

elia_go_order_2.speaker: Elia
elia_go_order_2.text: [i]Her hand finds the hilt of her blade. The ritual begins.[/i] We go quiet. We go fast. We go together. [i][She inhales. Holds it.][/i]

end_briefing.speaker: Narration
end_briefing.text: [i]The airlock opens. The station waits. Seven people step forward into the dark—armed, afraid, and unwilling to let fear make the choice for them. The floor holds. The floor always holds. Until it doesn't. And then you find out what you're really made of.[/i]

choices.text: [Continue]
